content.claim: Hungary demonstrates exceptionally high between-school variance (71% of reading performance variation vs. OECD average 36%), indicating extreme stratification by school
content.context: Fresh live research through the International comparison lens
limitations: Cross-sectional snapshot; does not establish whether tracking caused this variance or pre-existed it
Between-school variance reflects both tracking design and free school choice interactions
No direct comparison of Hungary's between-school variance before vs. after early selection was formally introduced
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
